metadata.summary: Provider-specific embedding API integration patterns for OpenAI, Voyage AI, Cohere, local sentence-transformers, and Mistral — async, asymmetric, dimension reduction, GPU acceleration.

content.title: Embedding Provider API Integration
content.summary: Production-ready integration patterns for the four main embedding provider categories: OpenAI (symmetric, Matryoshka dims), Voyage AI (asymmetric, quantization), Cohere (multimodal, input types, multilingual), and local sentence-transformers (GPU, BGE-M3 dense+sparse). Each provider requires a different client setup and has distinct capabilities.

why.p: Agents embed in two phases: indexing (one-time or incremental batch) and query-time (per request). The embedding model must be the same for both phases — agents must validate model identity before upsert. Using the wrong input_type for query vs document (Cohere and Voyage) drops retrieval quality 3-8%. Silent truncation at token limits changes semantic meaning without raising an error — always count tokens before embedding.

list.item: Building a new embedding pipeline and need provider-specific client code.
Implementing asymmetric search (different representation for query vs document).
Adding GPU acceleration for local models or quantized output for storage savings.
Integrating multimodal embeddings (text + image in the same vector space).
When you need a unified multi-provider abstraction — use litellm instead of implementing per-provider wrappers.
When you have not yet selected a model — see embeddings-model-selection first.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Agents embed in two phases: indexing (one-time or incremental batch) and query-time (per request). For indexing, the agent iterates the document corpus in batches of 100-2048 items, calls the embedding API, and upserts vectors into the vector DB. For query-time, the agent embeds the user query (single call) and searches the vector DB. The embedding model must be the same for both phases — agents should validate model identity before upsert. Use asymmetric input_type when the provider supports it.

section.name: openai-integration
section.p: OpenAI embedding API: symmetric (no input types), supports Matryoshka dimension reduction, batch up to 2048 texts per request. Store as float32 in Qdrant/Weaviate — OpenAI returns float64 by default, halving storage has negligible quality impact.

example.title: OpenAI single and batch embedding with dimension reduction
example.code:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI() # Uses OPENAI_API_KEY env var
def get_embedding(text: str, model: str = "text-embedding-3-large") -> list[float]: """Get embedding for a single text.""" response = client.embeddings.create(input=text, model=model) return response.data[0].embedding
def get_embedding_reduced( text: str, model: str = "text-embedding-3-large", dimensions: int = 256 ) -> list[float]: """Get reduced-dimension embedding — 12x smaller with ~4% quality loss.""" response = client.embeddings.create(input=text, model=model, dimensions=dimensions) return response.data[0].embedding
def get_embeddings_batch( texts: list[str], model: str = "text-embedding-3-large", batch_size: int = 2048, # OpenAI limit dimensions: int | None = None ) -> list[list[float]]: """Get embeddings for multiple texts efficiently.""" all_embeddings = [] for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size): batch = texts[i:i + batch_size] kwargs = {"input": batch, "model": model} if dimensions: kwargs["dimensions"] = dimensions response = client.embeddings.create(**kwargs) sorted_embeddings = sorted(response.data, key=lambda x: x.index) all_embeddings.extend([e.embedding for e in sorted_embeddings]) return all_embeddings

example.title: OpenAI async batch with controlled concurrency
example.code:
import asyncio from openai import AsyncOpenAI
async_client = AsyncOpenAI()
async def get_embeddings_async( texts: list[str], model: str = "text-embedding-3-large", batch_size: int = 100, max_concurrent: int = 5 ) -> list[list[float]]: """Process large volumes with controlled concurrency.""" semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrent)
async def process_batch(batch: list[str], batch_idx: int) -> tuple[int, list[list[float]]]: async with semaphore: response = await async_client.embeddings.create(input=batch, model=model) sorted_data = sorted(response.data, key=lambda x: x.index) return batch_idx, [e.embedding for e in sorted_data]
batches = [texts[i:i+batch_size] for i in range(0, len(texts), batch_size)] tasks = [process_batch(batch, idx) for idx, batch in enumerate(batches)] results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks) sorted_results = sorted(results, key=lambda x: x[0]) return [emb for _, batch_embs in sorted_results for emb in batch_embs]

section.name: voyage-ai-integration
section.p: Voyage AI: SOTA retrieval quality, shared embedding space, MoE architecture. Supports asymmetric input_type (document vs query), Matryoshka dimensions, and quantization (float/int8/binary). Newer ecosystem, limited enterprise integrations.

example.title: Voyage AI asymmetric search with quantization
example.code:
import voyageai
client = voyageai.Client() # Uses VOYAGE_API_KEY env var
def get_voyage_embedding( text: str, model: str = "voyage-3.5", input_type: str = "document" ) -> list[float]: result = client.embed(texts=[text], model=model, input_type=input_type) return result.embeddings[0]
def get_voyage_embeddings_batch( texts: list[str], model: str = "voyage-3.5", input_type: str = "document", output_dimension: int = 1024, # Matryoshka: 256, 512, 1024, 2048 output_dtype: str = "float" # "float", "int8", "binary" ) -> list[list[float]]: """Batch embeddings with dimension reduction and quantization.""" result = client.embed( texts=texts, model=model, input_type=input_type, output_dimension=output_dimension, output_dtype=output_dtype ) return result.embeddings
# Asymmetric search — use correct input_type per side doc_embedding = get_voyage_embedding("Python is a programming language", input_type="document") query_embedding = get_voyage_embedding("What is Python?", input_type="query")
# 75% smaller vectors: int8 + 512 dims embeddings = get_voyage_embeddings_batch( texts=["Doc 1", "Doc 2"], output_dimension=512, output_dtype="int8" )

example.title: Voyage contextualized chunks (voyage-context-3)
example.code:
def embed_contextualized_chunks( chunks: list[str], document_context: str, model: str = "voyage-context-3" ) -> list[list[float]]: """Embed chunks with full document context prepended.""" contextualized = [ f"Document context: {document_context}\n\nChunk: {chunk}" for chunk in chunks ] result = client.embed(texts=contextualized, model=model, input_type="document") return result.embeddings
document = "This is a long document about machine learning..." chunks = ["Neural networks", "Gradient descent", "Backpropagation"] embeddings = embed_contextualized_chunks(chunks, document[:500])

section.name: cohere-integration
section.p: Cohere: multimodal (embed-v4 handles text + images in same vector space), 128K token context, 100+ languages. Input types: search_document, search_query, classification, clustering, image. Higher latency, complex pricing.

example.title: Cohere asymmetric and multilingual embeddings
example.code:
import cohere
co = cohere.Client() # Uses COHERE_API_KEY env var
def get_cohere_embedding( texts: list[str], input_type: str = "search_document", model: str = "embed-v4" ) -> list[list[float]]: """ input_type options: - "search_document": documents to be indexed - "search_query": search queries - "classification": classification tasks - "clustering": clustering tasks - "image": image embeddings (embed-v4 only) """ response = co.embed( texts=texts, model=model, input_type=input_type, truncate="END" ) return response.embeddings
# Asymmetric search doc_embeddings = get_cohere_embedding(["Python guide", "Java tutorial"], "search_document") query_embedding = get_cohere_embedding(["programming language"], "search_query")[0]
# Multilingual — same embedding space for 100+ languages def get_multilingual_embeddings(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: response = co.embed(texts=texts, model="embed-multilingual-v3.0", input_type="search_document") return response.embeddings
multilingual = get_multilingual_embeddings(["Hello world", "Bonjour le monde", "Hola mundo"])

example.title: Cohere embed-v4 multimodal (text + image)
example.code:
import base64 from pathlib import Path
def embed_image(image_path: str) -> list[float]: """Embed an image with Cohere embed-v4.""" image_bytes = Path(image_path).read_bytes() image_b64 = base64.standard_b64encode(image_bytes).decode("utf-8") response = co.embed(model="embed-v4", input_type="image", images=[image_b64]) return response.embeddings[0]
# Cross-modal search: text and image in same vector space text_emb = get_cohere_embedding(["A cat sitting on a couch"], "search_document")[0] image_emb = embed_image("cat_photo.jpg") # These can be compared directly with cosine similarity

section.name: local-models-integration
section.p: Local models via sentence-transformers: free, private, customizable, GPU-accelerated. Requires infrastructure. 15,000+ pre-trained models on HuggingFace. BGE-M3 provides both dense and sparse (lexical) embeddings for native hybrid search.

example.title: Local sentence-transformers with GPU auto-detection
example.code:
import torch from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu" model = SentenceTransformer("BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5", device=device)
def get_local_embeddings( texts: list[str], batch_size: int = 32 ) -> list[list[float]]: embeddings = model.encode( texts, batch_size=batch_size, show_progress_bar=True, convert_to_numpy=True, normalize_embeddings=True # cosine similarity equals dot product ) return embeddings.tolist()
# Popular model presets # Best quality (slow, 8K context): BAAI/bge-m3 # Good quality, balanced: BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 # Fast inference: sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 # Multilingual: sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 # Long context 8K + MoE: nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v2-moe (trust_remote_code=True) # Instruction-aware: Alibaba-NLP/gte-Qwen2-7B-instruct (trust_remote_code=True)

example.title: BGE-M3 dense + sparse for hybrid search
example.code:
from FlagEmbedding import BGEM3FlagModel
model = BGEM3FlagModel("BAAI/bge-m3", use_fp16=True)
def get_bge_m3_embeddings( texts: list[str], return_sparse: bool = False ) -> dict: """Get BGE-M3 dense and optionally sparse embeddings.""" output = model.encode(texts, return_dense=True, return_sparse=return_sparse) result = {"dense": output["dense_vecs"].tolist()} if return_sparse: result["sparse"] = output["lexical_weights"] return result
# Hybrid search: dense + sparse result = get_bge_m3_embeddings(["Python programming", "Java development"], return_sparse=True) dense_embeddings = result["dense"] sparse_embeddings = result["sparse"] # For BM25-style matching

example.title: Mistral embedding basic usage
example.code:
from mistralai import Mistral
client = Mistral(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
def get_mistral_embedding(text: str) -> list[float]: response = client.embeddings.create(model="mistral-embed", inputs=[text]) return response.data[0].embedding
def get_mistral_embeddings_batch(texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: response = client.embeddings.create(model="mistral-embed", inputs=texts) return [e.embedding for e in response.data]
embedding = get_mistral_embedding("Hello world") print(f"Dimensions: {len(embedding)}") # 1024

list.item: openai — OpenAI text-embedding-3-* API client. Install: pip install openai.
voyageai — Voyage AI embedding client. Install: pip install voyageai.
cohere — Cohere embed-v4, multilingual. Install: pip install cohere.
sentence-transformers — Local models (BGE-M3, MiniLM). Install: pip install sentence-transformers.
fastembed — Fast CPU-optimized local embeddings (Qdrant). Install: pip install fastembed.
litellm — Unified embedding API across providers. Install: pip install litellm.

rule.statement: Always use the same model version for indexing and query — even a minor version bump can produce incompatible embeddings requiring full re-indexing.
rule.rationale: Embedding spaces are model-specific. A version bump changes the geometry — mixing old and new vectors in the same index produces unpredictable similarity scores.

rule.statement: Token counting before embedding is mandatory: silent truncation at the model's token limit (e.g. 8192) changes semantic meaning without raising an error.
rule.rationale: When a document exceeds the token limit, the embedding reflects only the truncated portion. The agent will retrieve based on incomplete content, leading to quality degradation that is hard to diagnose.

rule.statement: Normalize embeddings before storage (normalize_embeddings=True in sentence-transformers). Cosine similarity then equals dot product, enabling faster ANN algorithms.
rule.rationale: L2-normalized vectors enable inner product search instead of full cosine computation, which is the standard in FAISS IndexFlatIP and Qdrant with DOT distance — often 2-3x faster at query time.

antipattern.description: Cache query embeddings keyed only by text when the model changes.
antipattern.reason: Include model identifier in the cache key: f"{model}:{text}". A model change invalidates all cached embeddings silently otherwise.

antipattern.description: Embedding HTML or markdown with tags intact.
antipattern.reason: Strip formatting before embedding or the model wastes token budget on structure tokens (div, span, **, ##) instead of semantic content, reducing embedding quality.

antipattern.description: Forgetting input_type="query" for search queries when using Voyage or Cohere, defaulting to "document".
antipattern.reason: Using document embedding for queries drops retrieval quality 3-8% because query and document representations are optimized for different linguistic patterns in asymmetric models.
